_doc: NVIDIA 'AI 5-layer cake' buildout tracker (blogs.nvidia.com/blog/ai-5-layer-cake). Layers bottom→top: Energy, Chips, Infrastructure, Models, Applications. Every application pulls on every layer beneath it; the binding constraint (bottleneck) migrates over time and capital follows it. momentum & bottleneck_tightness are 0-100. is_primary_bottleneck flags where the constraint currently binds. Mostly editorial (confidence + is_free badge the auditable numbers); a few headline metrics can be auto-updated by the pipeline. Maintain by editing this file. Values as of mid-2026.
thesis: 2023–24 the binding constraint was chips; through 2025–26 it moved down the stack to energy and infrastructure (grid interconnect, transformers, construction labor). Capital concentrates wherever the bottleneck sits; as power loosens (2027+), value migrates up to models and applications.

layers.name: Energy
layers.subtitle: power generation — the binding constraint
layers.whats_happening: Hyperscalers now spend 60%+ of capex on power, not chips; PPAs, nuclear/SMR deals and behind-the-meter gas racing to add generation.
layers.bottleneck: Grid interconnect queues hold ~2,300 GW with 4–7 yr waits; transformer lead times ~128 weeks. Generation, not demand, is the ceiling.
layers.investment_note: Tightest layer in the stack — IPPs, grid-equipment (transformers/switchgear) and SMR suppliers ride the constraint.

layers.name: Chips
layers.subtitle: compute — GPUs, HBM, advanced packaging
layers.whats_happening: GPU rental spot prices still rising even for two-generations-old parts; custom silicon (TPU, Trainium, Maia) scaling alongside NVIDIA.
layers.bottleneck: Not wafers but HBM and CoWoS packaging — HBM makers pre-sold their entire 2026 capacity. Loosening vs the 2024 GPU crunch.
layers.investment_note: Binding constraint has migrated down to power; chips are hot but no longer the single ceiling. HBM/packaging suppliers still lucrative.

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.subtitle: AI factories — land, cooling, networking, construction
layers.whats_happening: The largest infrastructure buildout in history — Big-4 hyperscaler capex ~$725B in 2026, +77% YoY, with >$1T expected in 2027.
layers.bottleneck: Construction-to-energized lag: skilled trades short and long-lead electrical gear; ~16 GW announced 2026 capacity vs ~5 GW under construction.
layers.investment_note: Capital concentrated here now — cooling, networking, neoclouds and electrical-gear suppliers ride the constraint.

layers.name: Models
layers.subtitle: frontier & open-weight foundation models
layers.whats_happening: Reasoning up, hallucinations down; competitive release cadence collapsed to a 4–6 week floor; most of the world's models are now open-weight and free.
layers.bottleneck: Not supply-constrained — the pressure is economic: commoditization via open weights, training-cost ROI, and margin compression.
layers.investment_note: Constraint here is margin, not compute. Differentiation shifts to product, distribution and proprietary data.

layers.name: Applications
layers.subtitle: where economic value is created — agents, copilots, robotics
layers.whats_happening: Fastest revenue growth off a small base — coding agents ~$10B annualized; inference cost falling 9×–900×/yr unlocks agentic workflows.
layers.bottleneck: Inference unit economics, trust/reliability and ROI proof — token prices fell ~280× in two years yet deployment ROI is the real gate.
layers.investment_note: As lower-layer bottlenecks ease (2027+), value migrates up here — vertical agents with proven ROI are the next opportunity.
